prompt: This function should: - Splits the input data into training (70%) and test (30%) sets. - Constructs a Keras Sequential model with one hidden dense layer and sigmoid activation. The input dimension is determined based on the first feature set of X. - Compiles the model using binary cross-entropy loss and SGD optimizer. - Fits the model to the training data in a non-verbose mode. - Plots the Precision-Recall curve for the model based on the test set data. Note that: The plot's x-axis is labeled 'Recall', and the y-axis is labeled 'Precision'. The title of the axes is set to 'Precision-Recall Curve'. The axes object allows for further customization of the plot outside the function. The function should output with: tuple(trained_model: keras.models.Sequential, pr_curve_axes: matplotlib.axes._axes.Axes) trained_model: The trained Keras model. pr_curve_axes: The matplotlib Axes object for the Precision-Recall curve plot. You should write self-contained code starting with: ``` from sklearn.model_selection import train_test_split from sklearn.metrics import precision_recall_curve from tensorflow import keras import matplotlib.pyplot as plt def task_func(X, Y): ```
